The package covers 4 × 700 MWe indigenous Pressurised Heavy Water Reactors (PHWRs) and includes engineering, manufacturing, supply, construction, installation, and commissioning support for critical nuclear systems. highlights: - Largest Nuclear Island EPC package in India's indigenous nuclear programme - Project to be tendered by Anushakti Vidhyut Nigam Limited, a joint venture of Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited and NTPC Limited - Supports India's target of 100 GW nuclear capacity by 2047 - Expected to strengthen domestic manufacturing and boost clean, low-carbon power generation. tweets.content:
BREAKING: India Floats ₹28,000 Crore Nuclear EPC Tender

India is set to issue its largest-ever Nuclear Island EPC tender, worth over ₹28,000 crore, for the Mahi Banswara Rajasthan Atomic Power Project (MBRAPP).

The package covers 4 × 700 MWe indigenous Pressurised Heavy Water Reactors (PHWRs) and includes engineering, manufacturing, supply, construction, installation, and commissioning support for critical nuclear systems.

highlights:

- Largest Nuclear Island EPC package in India's indigenous nuclear programme
- Project to be tendered by Anushakti Vidhyut Nigam Limited, a joint venture of Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited and NTPC Limited
- Supports India's target of 100 GW nuclear capacity by 2047
- Expected to strengthen domestic manufacturing and boost clean, low-carbon power generation.

India currently has 8.78 GW of installed nuclear capacity, which is projected to reach 22.38 GW by 2031–32.

tweets.title: US Senate’s New Russia Sanctions Bill. What It Means for India A bipartisan Senate bill unveiled Tuesday (framed as a tribute to the late Sen. Lindsey Graham) proposes tariffs of up to 100% on the top 5 buyers of Russian oil/gas: China, India, Slovakia, Hungary, Azerbaijan. ✅ White House-backed, Could reach a vote before August per Sen. Blumenthal. Key mechanics: ✅ Exact tariff rate (0-100%) to be set by USTR, calibrated to “discourage” continued Russian energy purchases ✅ Exemption for countries importing less than 15% of Russia’s total gas exports AND actively reducing that share ✅ Also hits Russia’s shadow fleet, energy projects, financial institutions Context matters here. This is a softened version of the 2025 Sanctioning Russia Act, which proposed up to 500% tariffs and never got a vote, lacked Trump’s support then. This version has WH backing. That’s the real shift. India’s Russian crude exposure, recent trend: ✅ June 2026: imports hit a record ~2.35 mbpd (full month), touching 2.6 mbpd on some days : highest ever ✅ Russian share of India’s crude basket: as much as 53.5% in June ✅ Preceded by a dip : share fell below 25% between Dec’25-Feb’26 amid sanctions/payment friction, inflows down to ~1.0 mbpd Why the June spike: a US sanctions waiver issued in March (has impact or not need to be studied) after Hormuz tensions pushed crude past $100/bbl, let Indian refiners resume Russian purchases at scale. India remains the world’s second-largest buyer of Russian hydrocarbons. India-US trade exposure (FY26, Apr-Nov’25): ✅ Bilateral trade: $94.39B (record for the period) ✅ India’s exports to US: $58.99B ✅ Trade surplus with US: $23.59B US is India’s #1 export destination.
tweets.content:
US Senate’s New Russia Sanctions Bill.
What It Means for India

A bipartisan Senate bill unveiled Tuesday (framed as a tribute to the late Sen. Lindsey Graham) proposes tariffs of up to 100% on the top 5 buyers of Russian oil/gas: China, India, Slovakia, Hungary, Azerbaijan.
✅ White House-backed, Could reach a vote before August per Sen. Blumenthal.

Key mechanics:
✅ Exact tariff rate (0-100%) to be set by USTR, calibrated to “discourage” continued Russian energy purchases
✅ Exemption for countries importing less than 15% of Russia’s total gas exports AND actively reducing that share
✅ Also hits Russia’s shadow fleet, energy projects, financial institutions

Context matters here. This is a softened version of the 2025 Sanctioning Russia Act, which proposed up to 500% tariffs and never got a vote, lacked Trump’s support then. This version has WH backing. That’s the real shift.

India’s Russian crude exposure, recent trend:
✅ June 2026: imports hit a record ~2.35 mbpd (full month), touching 2.6 mbpd on some days : highest ever
✅ Russian share of India’s crude basket: as much as 53.5% in June
✅ Preceded by a dip : share fell below 25% between Dec’25-Feb’26 amid sanctions/payment friction, inflows down to ~1.0 mbpd

Why the June spike: a US sanctions waiver issued in March (has impact or not need to be studied) after Hormuz tensions pushed crude past $100/bbl, let Indian refiners resume Russian purchases at scale. India remains the world’s second-largest buyer of Russian hydrocarbons.

India-US trade exposure (FY26, Apr-Nov’25):
✅ Bilateral trade: $94.39B (record for the period)
✅ India’s exports to US: $58.99B
✅ Trade surplus with US: $23.59B
US is India’s #1 export destination.

tweets.title: RT by @sidhant: The Swastika is one of the oldest known sacred symbols in the world. In Hinduism, it represents auspiciousness, well-being, prosperity, and the eternal cycle of life, and has been used for thousands of years in religious rituals, temples, and festivals. Interestingly, swastika motifs have also been discovered on Bronze Age artifacts from Garajamirli in Azerbaijan's Shamkir district, reflecting the widespread use of this ancient symbol across Eurasia in prehistoric times. These findings underscore the rich cultural interactions and shared symbolic traditions that connected ancient societies across vast regions. They invite us to explore the deep historical links and exchanges that existed between the peoples of the Indian subcontinent and the South Caucasus. @PMOIndia @DrSJaishankar @iccr_hq @AmbSibiGeorge @incredibleindia @IndiaatUNESCO @MEAIndia @ASIGoI @AmbassadorAbhay

The Swastika is one of the oldest known sacred symbols in the world. In Hinduism, it represents auspiciousness, well-being, prosperity, and the eternal cycle of life, and has been used for thousands of years in religious rituals, temples, and festivals.
Interestingly, swastika motifs have also been discovered on Bronze Age artifacts from Garajamirli in Azerbaijan's Shamkir district, reflecting the widespread use of this ancient symbol across Eurasia in prehistoric times.
These findings underscore the rich cultural interactions and shared symbolic traditions that connected ancient societies across vast regions. They invite us to explore the deep historical links and exchanges that existed between the peoples of the Indian subcontinent and the South Caucasus.

India has reiterated its support for Palestine’s bid for full United Nations membership and reaffirmed its longstanding backing for a two-state solution, where Israel and Palestine coexist in peace within internationally recognized borders.

The position was conveyed by MEA Secretary Sripriya Ranganathan at the Palestine Donor Group meeting in Brussels.

India also announced new development and humanitarian projects in Palestine, focusing on healthcare, education, rehabilitation, vocational training, and women's empowerment.

The statement reaffirms India’s longstanding policy of supporting a negotiated two-state solution alongside continued development assistance to the Palestinian people.

tweets.title: I heard someone argue that air-to-air missiles are ordered in tranches because they have a finite service life. That’s correct but that is only part of the story and from very user seller point of view. Take the Indian Air Force’s order for roughly 240 Astra missiles. Yes, a missile has a shelf life, and yes, if it is not kept in a temperature-controlled environment its life can reduce further. All of that is fair. But the real issue is: if you place missile orders with a clear long-term roadmap, the manufacturing ecosystem can plan properly. It knows how much to produce, when to deliver, and where to invest in infrastructure. It can build a little extra capacity to handle crisis situations, so you are not forced to look outside for emergency purchases when demand suddenly spikes. That is why a 20-year roadmap for missiles and manufacturing makes far more sense than simply ordering 240 today and then waiting to see when all the MKI are ready to carry them, or when the Mk1A fleet finally arrives. That should not be just procurement. It should be full lifecycle planning with proper phase out replacement etc built it. Instead we are arguing we will order it when MKI are modified or MK1A are delivered. This very thought process has placed IAF where it stands today.

🚨 Red Fort Blast: Delhi Court Orders Dignified Last Rites for Victims After Months of Investigation

📍Delhi court has permitted the NIA to cremate and dispose of the remains of 11 victims killed in the November 2025 Red Fort terror blast, more than seven months after the attack.

📍The court also allowed the disposal of the remains of suicide bomber Dr. Umer Un Nabi, who died after detonating the explosive laden vehicle.

📍The NIA informed the court that all required forensic examinations had been completed.

📍The court directed that all remains be disposed of with full dignity while respecting the religious beliefs of the victims. It also sought a compliance report from the NIA.

📍The NIA had earlier filed a 7,500 page chargesheet in the Red Fort terror blast case on May 14 as its investigation continues.
